series_description:
This records series documents audits and
reviews performed by the Office of
Institutional Compliance to monitor
quality assurance of departmental clinical
billing for each health service provider,
including governmental regulatory audit
activity for tracking, responding, and
reviewing Recovery Audit Contractor (RAC)
activity. Records may include but are not
limited to: the working papers that
support the audit; copies of medical
records; clinic notes; analysis worksheets;
findings; recommendations; final report;
documentation of re-payment to federal
programs as a result of incorrect billing;
and any other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_description:
This records series documents an
assessment or audit performed on a study,
a lab, or personnel within Office of
Regulated Non-Clinical Studies (ORNcS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_description:
This records series documents any
supporting documentation created and/or
used to justify and support legislative
appropriations requests by an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Legislative Appropriation
Requests (LAR) must be submitted
to the Texas State Publications
Depository Program per 13 TAC
3.3(a)(3)(A). Working files and
related documentation used in
creating the final plan are not
subject to archival review and may
be disposed of at the expiration of
the retention period.

series_description:
This records series documents any
document that officially initiates, rescinds,
or amends general office procedures.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
This records series documents any
document that initiates, rescinds, or
amends a regulation, policy, or procedure
that governs the programs, services, or
projects of an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents calendars,
appointment books or programs and
scheduling, or itinerary records
maintained by staff during business hours
that document appointments, itineraries,
and other activities of agency officials or
employees.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
CAUTION: A record of this type
purchased with personal funds but
used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work
activities may be a state record and
subject to this retention period. See
Open Records Decision 635 issued
in December 1995 by the Attorney
General. ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the
calendars, appointment, and
itinerary records of elected
officials, executive staff, board or
commission members, division
directors, and program heads
require archival review. Contact the
University Archives when these
records have met their retention
periods.

series_description:
This records series documents news, press
releases, marketing files and collateral, or
any public relations files maintained or
issued by an agency. Records may include
but are not limited to those in: print;
electronic; audio; and audiovisual record
formats.

This records series documents all
correspondence and documentation
relating to requests for records that are
furnished to the public under the Public
Information Act (Chapter 552,
Government Code), including withdrawn
requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
This records series documents all
correspondence and documentation
relating to requests for records that are
excepted under the Public Information Act
(Chapter 552, Government Code),
including records provided to the
legislature under Section 552.008.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
This records series documents charts or
diagrams that show the structure of an
agency and the relationships and relative
ranks of its departments and job positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts
showing the overall arrangement
and administrative structure of the
state agency need to be transferred
to the University Archives.
Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail are
not considered archival.

series_description:
This records series documents
organizational charts that show lines of
authority and responsibility, department-
wide, within and between the various
divisions of Office of Regulated Non-
Clinical Studies (ORNcS).
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 10 years

series_description:
This records series documents UTMB
submissions to be included in the Texas
Register (the journal of state agency
rulemaking) to become a part of the Texas
Administrative Code (TAC). Records may
include but are not limited to: copies of all
proposed, withdrawn, emergency, and
adopted rules; notices of state agency
review of agency rules; open meetings
notices; or any other documents required
by law to be submitted to the Texas
Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
This records series documents drafts of
proposed legislation and related
correspondence.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
This records series documents notes or
text of speeches, papers, presentations, or
reports delivered in conjunction with
agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches,
papers, and presentations given by
or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members,
division directors, and program
heads require archival review.

series_description:
This records series documents operational
strategic plans, which include descriptions
of information resources programs,
prepared in accordance with Government
Code, 2054.095 and 2056.002. Records
may include working files and related
documentation used in creating the final
plan.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of Strategic Plans must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program
per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation
used in creating the final plan are
not subject to archival review and
may be disposed of at the
expiration of the retention period.

series_title:
Meetings, Agendas, and
Minutes - Alumni
Association Board
series_description:
This records series documents the
activities of the alumni association board
of directors or Board of Trustees. The
board is responsible for promoting the
interests and ideals of the institution;
stimulating and encouraging school pride
in students, graduates, and former
students; and developing a sense of
responsibility for continued progress in
educational programs for the institution.
Records may include but are not limited
to: agendas; minutes; reports; notes;
working papers; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
This records series documents biennial
narrative reports to the governor and
legislature as required by an agency's
enabling statutes. Records may include but
are not limited to: annual narrative
reports if they are required by statute;
working files; and related documentation
used in creating final report.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version
of these agency reports must be
submitted to the Texas State
Publications Depository Program,
Texas State Library and Archives
Commission (TSLAC), per 13 TAC
3.3. Working files and related
documentation used in creating the
final report may be disposed of at
the expiration of the retention
period.

series_description:
This records series documents reports
compiled by agency personnel on a daily
or other periodic basis pertaining to
workload monitoring, task completion
times, number of public contacts, records
showing computer jobs to be run,
computer output production, and similar
activities.

This records series documents reports
made to the Office of Attorney General
(OAG) on an agency's Public Information
Act activities, per Government Code,
552.010.

This records series documents transcripts
and final decisions of hearings conducted
as part of the regulatory process, and
hearings on proposed rules and changes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: These records may be
maintained with related
information, including meeting
notices, proofs of publication, and
meeting minutes that might require
a longer retention period.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only records that
lead to substantial or statutory
changes to the operations of the
agency and its policies and
procedures should be retained for
archival review.

series_title:
Sunset Review Report and
Related Documentation
series_description:
This records series documents the periodic
review of a state agency by the Sunset
Advisory Commission. In their report, they
make recommendations to the legislature
on the need for, performance of, and
improvements to an agency under their
Sunset Review. Records may include but
are not limited to: Sunset Review Report;
agency self-study or evaluation; and any
other related documentation and
correspondence connected to the Sunset
Review process.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related
documentation includes the agency
self-evaluation report, agency
response to the commission's draft
report, and documentation of the
agency's implementation of the
commission's final
recommendations.

series_description:
This records series documents the final
agreement described by Government
Code, 2009.054(c), associated with a
matter conducted under an alternative
dispute resolution procedure in which
personnel of a state agency participated as
a party on the agency's behalf.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
legal_citation:
Texas Civil Practice
and Remedies Code,
154.071.

series_description:
This records series documents copyright
records which pertain to the protection
that may be obtained for UTMB
employees' original work created within
the scope of his/her employment. Records
may include but are not limited to: a copy
of the work itself as submitted to the
United States Copyright Office; the
corresponding copyright application;
registration notice; supplementary
documents; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Deeds and Other Legal
Documents Reflecting
Legal Title
series_description:
This records series documents UTMB
property deeds and institution ownership
of vehicles such as cars, vans, trucks,
trailers, boats, tractors, and farm vehicles.
Records may include but are not limited
to: appraisals; surveys; descriptions of
property; the names of grantors and
grantees; mortgagers and mortgagees;
and any other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: Life of Asset

series_description:
This records series documents institutional-
paid individual memberships and activities
in professional organizations. Records may
include but are not limited to: applications
for membership; certification of
membership; record of activities; and any
other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_title:
Compliance / Reported
Allegations, Investigations,
and Reviews
series_description:
This records series documents United
States Federal Sentencing Guidelines that
mandate the requirements for an effective
compliance program, including a
mechanism to report allegations of wrong
doing, investigations of reported
allegations, and conducting assurance
activities. Records may include but are not
limited to: allegations reported to the
Fraud, Abuse and Privacy hotline;
assurance activity documentation;
supporting documentation from the
Institutional Compliance review; final
report; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 10 years

series_description:
This records series documents the form of
intellectual property protection that may
be obtained for a word, phrase, symbol, or
design (or combination thereof) that
identifies and distinguishes the source of a
service. Records may include but are not
limited to: a copy of the trademark
registration; the trademark application;
and correspondence among UTMB
officials, the inventors of the trademark,
and attorneys responsible for obtaining
trademark protection.
retention_statement: Permanent
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
This records series documents master
versions of all forms used internally and
externally by UTMB, including subsequent
revisions, any associated design or design
modification requests, or periodic listing of
all forms used.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
This records series documents forms used
to track the transmittal of records to the
University Records Center for storage.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
This records series documents logs or
similar records listing records destroyed or
transferred to the University Archives,
which might include records series title,
dates of records, and date destroyed or
transferred.
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean
destroyed or transferred.

series_description:
This records series documents worksheets
or working papers used to capture records
inventory information, including location
tracking and to document existence and
requirements of a records series.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
This records series documents records
used to provide and improve access to
records. Records may include but are not
limited to: indexes; card files; shelf lists;
registers; guides; registry book; and other
finding aids.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry
the same retention period and
archival code of the records they
support.

series_title:
Records Access and
Locator Aids - Electronic
Number Registry
series_description:
This records series documents records
used to identify and track medical record
and patient numbers. Data collected may
include but is not limited to: name; social
security number; date of birth; sex;
maiden name; address; and any other
basic demographic information about the
patient.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
CAUTION: These records must carry
the same retention period and
archival code of the records they
support.

series_description:
This records series documents transmittals
and related service documentation for
disaster recovery services provided by
TSLAC or other entities.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year

series_description:
This records series documents forms used
by TSLAC or other entities to establish
disaster recovery services, authorize
agency staff to access the media, and any
other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents requests by
UTMB offices for retrieval of boxes/files
stored at the University Records Center.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
This records series documents information
regarding the records series that is
authorized for storage at the University
Records Center.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Forms should remain on
file as long as the supplementing
records are being stored at the
University Records Center.

series_description:
This records series documents information
in any format that is publicly distributed
and produced by the authority of or
expense of UTMB, or is required to be
distributed by law. Records may include
but are not limited to: UTMB newsletters;
magazines; posters; brochures; handouts;
pamphlets; catalogs and bulletins of
academic policies/procedures and course
information; or any other type of material
that publicizes the departments, services,
research, programs, or functions of UTMB.
One copy of each state publication must
be maintained, except a publication that is
subject to a different retention period in
this schedule.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications
must be submitted to the Texas
State Publications Depository
Program, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission (TSLAC), per
Government Code, 441.103-
441.105. TSLAC will retain a copy of
all state publications submitted to
it on a continuing basis, subject to
periodic evaluation to determine if
the publication merits further
retention. For additional
information and requirements
concerning state publications made
available to the public through the
Internet, consult the administrative
rules listed per 13 TAC 3.1 - 3.8.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Transfer a copy of
all catalogs or bulletins to the
University Archives upon
publication.

series_description:
This records series documents relatively
long-lived computer files containing
organized and consistent sets of complete
and accurate electronic records and
associated processing files. Records may
include data tables of relational databases
used by applications or computer
programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management
officers must be certain before
assigning the retention period of AC
to agency master files and
application data that the files do
not fall under records series listed
elsewhere.

series_description:
This records series documents agency-
developed automated software
applications, operating system files, and
associated processing files. Records may
include but are not limited to: job control
language; programs; applications; scripts;
source code; and similar records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Software needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
This records series documents operational
and maintenance requirements of
computer hardware. Records may include
but are not limited to: operating manuals;
hardware/operating system requirements;
hardware configurations; equipment
control systems; and associated
processing files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware needed for
access to electronic records must
be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
This records series documents records
adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing electronic records, for making
modifications to computer programs, to
accessing, retrieving, manipulating, and
interpreting data in an automated system,
and to allow for their timely, authorized
disposition. Records may include but are
not limited to: user guides; system or sub-
system definitions; system specifications;
input and output specifications; system
flow charts; program flow charts; program
maintenance logs; change notices; data
element dictionaries; file layouts; code
books or tables; and other records that
explain the meaning, purpose, structure,
logical relationships, and origin of the data
elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware and software
needed for access to electronic
records must be retained for the
period of time required to access
the records.

series_description:
This records series documents the files
needed for electronic data audits. Records
may include but are not limited to: files or
reports showing transactions accepted,
rejected, suspended, and/or processed;
history files or tapes; records of updates
to application files; and security logs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents the files
needed for electronic data audits. Records
may include but are not limited to: files or
reports showing transactions accepted,
rejected, suspended, and/or processed;
history files or tapes; records of on-line
updates to application files; and security
logs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years

series_title:
Chargeback Records to
Data Processing Services
Users
series_description:
This records series documents the usage,
cost, billing, cost recovery, budgeting, and
administrative functions of computer
usage and data processing services for
individual units/departments/divisions at
UTMB.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 9 years
comments:
UTMB policy based on
requirements for auditing of
federal grant recipients and federal
cost report guidelines for hospitals
receiving Medicare reimbursement.
Currently data needed to support
these requirements are co-mingled
with data from non-federal
sources. Until a technological
solution exists that allows UTMB to
parse out the data based upon
funding sources, UTMB will need to
keep all data within this record
series.

series_description:
This records series documents forms and
logs used to enter and reconcile data sets
submitted for processing.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Quality Assurance Records
- Regulated Non-Clinical
Studies
series_description:
This records series documents information
that verifies the quality of system,
hardware, or software operations. Records
may include but are not limited to: records
of errors or failures and the loss of data
resulting from such failures;
documentation of abnormal termination
and of error free processing; checks of
changes put into production; transaction
histories; and other records needed as an
audit trail to evaluate data accuracy of
regulated non-clinical studies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties, and License
Agreements
series_description:
This records series documents the
registration and licensing of a software
application to activate the software for
legal use by the end users of a state
agency, along with warranties providing
that the software will perform in
accordance with functional specifications.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents the request
for and response to help desk tickets
received by divisions or units, such as
Information Services (IS).
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
This records series documents the biennial
information security plan for protecting
the security of the agency's information.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
This records series documents the
mechanisms used to monitor, identify, and
report electronic information security
breaches affecting personal computers
and networks. Records may include but
are not limited to: system tracking tickets;
supporting investigation documents; final
computer incident report; and any other
related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents the
applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents that
were submitted by candidates for vacant
positions who were hired.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
This records series documents internal or
external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.

This records series documents a contract
or agreement regarding the terms and
conditions of employment of an individual
and a contract with a vendor for
temporary staffing services that was
executed, renewed, or amended on or
after September 1, 2015. Also includes
performance bonds.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
This records series documents a contract
or agreement regarding the terms and
conditions of employment of an individual
and a contract with a vendor for
temporary staffing services that was
executed, renewed, or amended on or
before August 31, 2015. Also includes
performance bonds.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for
retention period context.

series_description:
This records series documents job
evaluations, performance appraisals, or
other similar documents used to evaluate
the performance of an employee.

This records series documents forms or
similar records used to create or change
information concerning the records of an
employee including pay grade, position
classification, employee number,
evaluation date, and termination of
employment.

This records series documents job
descriptions for positions at UTMB.
Records may include all associated task or
skill statements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
This records series documents an
employees' identity and authorization to
work in the United States on the federal
reporting form (Form I-9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents a summary
of apprenticeship applicant qualifications,
evaluation basis for selection or rejection,
original applications, job assignments,
promotions, separations, layoffs,
terminations, compensation records,
conditions of work, time records, hours of
training provided (separate from labor),
minority status, and gender of all selected
and rejected applicants.

This records series documents the
administrative approval process for new
positions within a department.
Information on the form may include but
is not limited to: the hiring manager name;
position; funding; budget information;
justification for the position; and approval
signatures.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 2 years

series_description:
This records series documents all class
information for each fitness and wellness
instructor. Records may include but are
not limited to: sign-in logs; course rosters;
skills checklists; written tests of students
and results; instructor evaluations; and
any other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents contracts,
recommendations, and letters from
people who have declined positions.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
Fiscal Year End plus 2 years

series_description:
This records series documents the
development of UTMB departments'
people plans appropriate for specified
department operations, incorporating the
University and the department's high-level
strategy, its people demand plus proposed
action plans to address any significant
discrepancies. Records may include but
are not limited to: Internal People
Planning Tool; Performance Improvement
Plan; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
Plans are completed at the close of
each fiscal year.

series_description:
This records series documents information
used to start, modify, or stop all voluntary
or required deductions from payroll,
including garnishment or other court-
ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
This records series documents payroll
records and registers documenting
employees earnings, wages, and pay.
Records may include but are not limited
to: payroll input records; summary
statements; payroll vouchers; payroll
detail sheets; and payroll history.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 4 years

series_description:
This records series documents a summary
record of data reported on the annual
wage and tax statements for agency
employees, corrections to these
statements, and a record of transmittal to
the federal government. Records may
include but are not limited to: print-outs
from the agency by year in social security
number order that includes names, social
security numbers, tax subject earnings,
and other data required by law; summary
transmittal forms; FICA records; IRS forms
1099, W2; and other tax records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years

series_description:
This records series documents increases or
decreases to employees' gross pay, FICA,
retirement, or in the computation of
taxes.

This records series documents employer's
copy of "Employee's Withholding
Exemption Certificate."
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
legal_citation:
IRS Circular E:
Employer's Tax
Guide; 26 CFR
31.6001-1(e)(2).

series_description:
This records series documents records
that define the wage or salary rate for
each position in the agency expressed in
dollars, grades, or step numbers.

This records series documents information
relating to unemployment compensation
claims, including reimbursement of funds
disbursed by Texas Workforce Commission
(TWC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
This records series documents the forms
used to deposit employee's earnings into a
specified personal account.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents the amount
of pension or deferred compensation
earned by individual employees.

This records series documents reports that
provide statistical information on the labor
force.

This records series documents records
relating to review and monitoring of job
classifications within an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents work, duty,
shift, crew, or case schedules, rosters, or
assignments.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
This records series documents work, duty,
shift, crew, or case schedules, rosters, or
assignments. Tracks training and workload
for those students enrolled in the
Specialist in Blood Banking (SBB) Program.

This records series documents reports to
the agency from TWC or its predecessor
pertaining to employees.

This records series documents work-
related activities for which the expenses of
the employee are defrayed or reimbursed.
Records may include but are not limited
to: requests and authorizations for travel;
participation in educational programs,
workshops, or college classes; or for other
similar activities.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents any reports
compiled by an agency on aspects of
personnel staffing. Information on reports
may include but is not limited to: listings
of all staff by program or name; staff hired
during a month; detailed listings of
employees within its organizational
structure, position vacancies, analyses of
turnover rates and seasonality of
employment; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents aptitude,
competency, or skills tests and checklists
required of job applicants or of current
personnel to qualify for promotion,
transfer, or continued employment in
current position, including validation
