not limited to: agendas; minutes; reports;
notes; protocol revisions; working papers;
and any other related documentation or
correspondence.

This records series documents the
authorization for use of audio or visual
media. Records may include the signed
consent form and any other related
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: If audio or visual
media records are archived, the
corresponding consent form needs
to be archived as well.

series_description:
This records series documents evaluations,
recommendations, and similar
documentation relating to the review
process for promotion and tenure for all
faculty or staff in the tenure track. Records
may include but are not limited to:
Appointment, Promotion, and Tenure
(APT) proposals; Chair's letter of proposal;
faculty curriculum vitae (CV); post-tenure
review recommendations; and any other
related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: Retention period may be
longer if tenure review cycle
requires it. For employment
performance and appraisal records,
see RRS Section 3.1.

series_title:
International Scholars
Records - Immigrant
Petition File
series_description:
This records series documents an
institution's sponsorship of an applicant
using United States Citizenship and
Immigration Services (USCIS) Form I-140
(Immigrant Petition for Alien Worker).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
This records series documents the
application and approval of international
scholars for permanent immigrant status.
Records may include but are not limited
to: Application for Permanent
Employment Certifications (Department of
Labor (DOL) Form ETA 9089); Application
for Alien Employment Certification (DOL
Form ETA 750); recruitment reports; copy
of job advertisement in national
professional journal; wage
determinations; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
International Scholars
Records - Nonimmigrant
Visa Petition Files
series_description:
This records series documents both
internal documents and forms submitted
and inspected by the U.S. Citizenship and
Immigration Services (USCIS) for
temporary employment of international
visitors (non-immigrants) by the institution
and is used to monitor compliance with
USCIS regulations. Records may include
Form I-129 and the petition for a
nonimmigrant worker; Labor Condition
Application (DOL Form 9035/9035E); wage
rate documentation; benefits summaries;
and related materials for H1-B, H-1B1, and
E-3 visas.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
This records series documents tax
documentation for international students'
and scholars' acquisition of social security
numbers and application forms to the
Internal Revenue Service (IRS) for
acquiring an Individual Taxpayer
Identification Number (ITIN). Records may
include but are not limited to: social
security number applications; statement
of Information - Social Security Account
Number forms; photocopies of social
security cards; copies of passport; INS
Form I-94; Visa Stamp and/or other
evidence of foreign residence; completed
IRS Form W-7; IRS Form W-8; IRS Form
8233; photocopy of IRS Form 9844 (ITIN
card); and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents a check to
see if all providers are eligible to
participate in the state and federal
healthcare programs (Medicare/Medicaid)
and the procurement process. The
exclusion check provides a monthly report
of those who cleared and did not clear the
exclusion check process.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_title:
International Scholars
Exchange Program
Administration Records
series_description:
This records series documents the
program which allows one or more visiting
scholars to assume residence on or off
campus for an academic year or a shorter
duration. Records may include but are not
limited to: international exchange visitor
selection records; pre-arrival information
provided to visitors; program orientation
materials; monitoring of visitors and their
dependents; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.

This records series documents potential or
realized private, corporate, or public
agency funding to the institution, including
major endowments and trusts. Records
may include but are not limited to: letters
and agreements of gifts; copies of bequest
instruments and wills from individuals or
estates; award guidelines; amount of
donations received; name of donors;
conditions placed on donations;
gift/pledge transmittal form;
acknowledgement letters; copies of
checks or securities documents; and any
other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
This records series documents the annual
reports that are required by UT System to
be sent to each donor documenting the
funds received and expended by
endowment accounts to a UTMB
department. Records of endowed student
scholarships may include but are not
limited to: thank you letters from students
to the donor; annual financial update from
UTIMCO on the earnings of the
endowment; donor reports; and any other
related documentation or
correspondence. Records of endowed
academic positions (professorships, chairs,
etc.) may include but are not limited to:
departmental reports on how the
endowment distributions were used for
the fiscal year; annual financial update
from UTIMCO on the earnings of the
endowment; donor reports; and any other
related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years

series_title:
Notification of Use (NOU)
of Biological and
Recombinant Material
series_description:
This records series documents the
Notification of Use (NOU) of Biological and
Recombinant Material is submitted to the
Institutional Biosafety Committee prior to
research commencing.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents Dual Use Research of
Concern (DURC), or life sciences research that,
based on current understanding, can be reasonably
anticipated to provide knowledge, information,
products, or technologies that could be directly
misapplied to pose a significant threat with broad
potential consequences to public health and safety,
agricultural crops and other plants, animals, the
environment, material, or national security. This series
covers all documents that should be evaluated by
institutions for possible risks, as well as benefits, in all
domains, to ensure that risks are appropriately
managed and benefits realized. Records may include
but are not limited to: Identification that research
involves one or more of the 15 agents or toxins;
assessment of agents or toxins; Principal Investigator
(PI) and Institutional Review Entity (IRE) working
papers; institutional review documents for assessing if
research uses one or more of the 15 toxins/agents;
determination and assessment of the seven effects;
DURC determination; risk assessment; identification
of anticipated benefits; risk mitigation plan; evaluation
and review of mitigation plan; results notification of the
review by the National Institute of Health (NIH) or
United States Government (USG) funding agency;
DURC training; IRE reviews of active risk mitigation
plans; notification of change in status to a DURC
project and details of change; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
legal_citation:
United States
Government Policy
for Institutional
Oversight of Life
Sciences Dual Use
Research of Concern,
Section 7.2(F).

series_description:
This records series documents institutional
compliance with environmental laws and
guidelines of federal, state, or local
governments, including communications
with the Regional Air Pollution Authority
(RAPA), which is under the jurisdiction of
the Environmental Protection Agency
(EPA) of the federal government, and also
the Texas Commission on Environmental
Quality (TCEQ). Records may include but
are not limited to: Title V Air Discharge
Permits; Air Quality Reports; Waste Water
Discharge Permit Applications; Waste
Water Discharge Permits; Water Quality
Reports; Waste Water Discharge Records;
Notices of Violation; Notices of Non-
compliance; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_title:
Chemical and Hazardous
Waste Disposal Records
series_description:
This records series documents the receipt,
shipment, and disposal of chemical
material or hazardous wastes on campus.
Records may include but are not limited
to: chemical and waste inspection forms;
drum packing sheets; Uniform Hazardous
Waste Manifest forms (EPA 8700);
Certificates of Disposal from vendors; land
disposal notification forms; waste disposal
records; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
This records series documents surveys
completed by lab staff or radiation safety
staff in regard to radioactive
contamination levels in the lab. Survey is
completed after each use of radioactive
material and on a monthly basis as
required by Radiation Safety guidelines.
Records may include but are not limited
to: routine surveys; instrument calibration
surveys; and package surveys.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents the
requirements for possessing, using, or
transferring select agents or toxins that
might have a potential to pose a severe
threat to public health and safety. Long
term storage inventory records may
include but are not limited to: name;
characteristics; quantity, date of
acquisition and source; storage location;
tracking of time of movement and by
whom; which agent used; purpose of use;
quantity used and by whom; and date,
sender, and recipient for intra-entity
transfers. If destroyed, records may
include but are not limited to: quantity of
toxin destroyed; date and by whom; a list
of all individuals granted access approval
from the Health and Human Services
secretary or administrator; information
about all entries into areas containing
select agents (must include name of
individual, name of escort, date and time);
and written explanations of any
discrepancies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents the test
done to assess the seal between the
respirator's facepiece and the face of the
employee wearing it. These tests are done
to ensure that personnel are medically
able to wear a respirator and that the
respirator fits properly and can protect
employees from exposure to hazardous
substances. Records may include but are
not limited to: qualitative and quantitative
fit tests; name of employee tested; type of
fit test performed; specific make, model,
and size of respirator tested; date test
administered; and results of test, fit factor,
strip chart, or any other recording of the
test results, as applicable.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents a
department level file for each imaging
equipment or machine. Records may
include but are not limited to: the
certificate of registration; receipt, transfer,
or disposal of a machine; training and
experience records on all personnel using
the machine; FDA variances, if any; current
operating and safety procedures for the
machine; personnel monitoring records on
individuals using the machine; public dose
evaluations/surveys; lead shielding
integrity check reports; R-F shielding
reports from vendor at installation;
advanced level training records; and any
other related documentation or
correspondence as specified by the
various regulations and accrediting bodies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: This series is a
consolidated file intended only for
use by the Radiology Department
to have a quick reference copy on
hand. Indivdiual records listed in
this series kept elsewhere might
have longer retention periods. See
RRS Section 5 for those retention
requirements.

series_description:
This records series documents the process
of ensuring the physical protection of
category 1 and 2 radioactive materials and
the areas on campus that hold them.
Records may include but are not limited
to: certification that each individual
employee's identification was properly
reviewed and all of the documents that
were used for that review; trustworthy
and reliability report; fingerprint reports;
criminal history records; background
checks; any other reference/history check;
verification of approval from a service
provider licensee; confirmation of receipt
for the right to correct background check
notice; background or criminal history
check consent form; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: At a minimum,
individual's access should be
reinvestigated every 10 years.

series_description:
This records series documents the denied
access authorizations to category 1 and 2
radioactive materials and the areas on
campus that hold them. Records may
include but are not limited to: certification
that each individual employee's
identification was properly reviewed and
the documents used to review;
trustworthy and reliability report;
fingerprint reports; criminal history
records; background checks; any other
reference/history check; confirmation of
receipt for the right to correct background
check notice; background or criminal
history check consent form; and any other
related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: There is a 1 year period
of time for an individual to contest
and correct any information to the
reports and background checks
received.

series_description:
This records series documents a current
list of all employees who have been
approved and granted authorization to
have unescorted access to category 1 and
2 radioactive materials and the areas
where they are located.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_title:
Radioactive Material
Handling and Disposal
Records
series_description:
This records series documents the
reception, handling, shipment, and
disposal of radioactive material or
radioactive hazardous wastes at the
institution to comply with federal and
state record keeping and reporting
requirements and to provide the office
with a record of past activity. Records may
include but are not limited to: waste
material pick up requests and tags;
Radioactive Waste Drum Inventory forms
(RS 102); Uniform Hazardous Waste
Manifest forms (EPA 8700-22); Radiation
Waste Shipment and Disposal Manifest
forms (vendor form); Radiation Material
Shipment Receipt Record forms (RS400);
Waste Disposal Record cards; disposal site
letters of arrival acknowledgment;
Sewered Radioactive Material log sheets;
and any other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_title:
Radioactive Material
Leakage or Contamination
Test
series_description:
This records series documents tests
conducted on sealed sources of
radioactive material to ensure there has
not been any leakage or contamination.

series_title:
Entrance Exposure Rate
(Air Kerma Rate) /
Fluoroscopy
series_description:
This records series documents periodic
measurements of entrance exposure rate
(air-kerma rate) for fluoroscopic systems
performed by a licensed Medical Physicist.
Results of measurements shall be posted
where any Fluoroscopist may have ready
access while using Fluoroscopic System.

series_title:
Resident Rank, Match, and
Placement Records
series_description:
This records series documents the process
of matching, ranking, and placing students
into programs, specifically as a part of the
National Resident Matching Program
(NRMP). Records may include but are not
limited to: written reference history of a
student to be forwarded to potential
employers or professional schools;
match/rank placement spreadsheets;
credentials records showing where, when,
and cost of letters sent; release of
information form which includes a listing
of the reference letters to be sent;
reference letters; student teaching
reports; professional program certificates;
personal data sheets and resumes; college
interview forms; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_title:
Resident Rank, Match, and
Placement Records - GME
Office
series_description:
This records series documents the process
of matching, ranking, and placing students
into programs, specifically as a part of the
National Resident Matching Program
(NRMP). Records may include but are not
limited to: written reference history of a
student to be forwarded to potential
employers or professional schools;
match/rank placement spreadsheets;
credentials records showing where, when,
and cost of letters sent; release of
information form which includes a listing
of the reference letters to be sent;
reference letters; student teaching
reports; professional program certificates;
personal data sheets and resumes; college
interview forms; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Advanced Placement and
Credit Records - Awarded
series_description:
This records series documents the
evaluation and determination to award
advanced placement or course credits to
students. Records may include but are not
limited to: transfer credit evaluations;
national or state standardized test scores
and reports (e.g., CLEP, AP, Departmental,
IB, SAT II, DSST); credit by examination
authorizations and reports; military course
documentation (e.g., USAFI) and
evaluations; non-traditional transcripts
and credentials; life experience records;
requests for advanced credit; and any
other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_title:
Advanced Placement and
Credit Records - Not
Awarded
series_description:
This records series documents the
evaluation and determination to deny
requests for transfer credits and/or
advanced placement. Records may include
but are not limited to: transfer credit
evaluations; national or state standardized
test scores and reports (e.g., CLEP, AP,
Departmental, IB, SAT II, DSST); credit by
examination authorizations and reports;
military course documentation (e.g.,
USAFI) and evaluations; non-traditional
transcripts and credentials; life experience
records; requests for advanced credit; and
any other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
This records series documents which
courses student are registered for at the
beginning of an academic term, including
documentation evidencing the conditions
under which courses are undertaken (e.g.,
audit, pass/fail, and credit/no credit
authorizations, or approvals) or evidencing
changes to registration status during the
term (e.g., add/drop forms and withdrawal
authorizations by student or instructor).
Records may include but are not limited
to: registration forms; class rosters; class
schedules; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
This records series documents student
requests to change their field of major
study, add or remove a simultaneous
major, or add or change a minor.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
This records series documents student
applications for graduation, degree audit
forms, graduation authorizations, and
diploma distribution.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
This records series documents records
used to place and remove holds on
registration, student services, or the
release of transcripts or other academic
data until the subject of the
hold/encumbrance takes a specific action.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents
departmental approval for students to
enroll in independent study courses.
Records may include but are not limited
to: permission sheets with students'
names; course names; number of credits;
and faculty signatures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
This records series documents records of
academic advisement to students. Records
may include but are not limited to:
advisors' notes; advising checklists; and
unofficial or partial copies of grade
reports.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents the
completion and academic acceptance of
graduate theses and dissertations
presented to colleges in fulfillment of
requirements for graduate degrees.
Records may include final and accepted
copies of theses and dissertations.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
This records series documents requests
and authorizations for a student to
withdraw from classes after calendar
deadlines without academic penalty for
reasons acceptable to UTMB, such as
health or family issues.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
This records series documents all records
related to research projects undertaken by
students in the course of their education
at UTMB. Records may include but are not
limited to: applications for research;
protocols; research progress; lab
notebooks; initial reports; evaluations by
faculty; IRB or IACUC approval and
monitoring; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_title:
Financial Aid Application
and Award Records - Not-
Awarded
series_description:
This records series documents the
application for and denial of financial aid
for all grants, scholarships, loans, or
opportunities to participate in work-study
programs. Records may include but are
not limited to (as applicable to specific
programs): applications; financial aid need
analysis and eligibility forms; financial aid
transcripts; selective service registration
compliance statements and any
supporting documentation; statements of
educational purpose; anti-drug abuse
statements; Student Aid Report (SAR) or
Institutional Student Information Report
(ISIR); authorization and award forms;
copies of documents submitted by
students or parents for verification of
student aid application information; and
any other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_title:
Financial Aid Application,
Award, and Disbursement
Records - Veterans
Administration Education
Assistance Allowances
series_description:
This records series documents the
application, award, and disbursement of
Veterans Administration (VA) benefits.
Records may include but are not limited to
(as applicable to specific programs):
financial aid need analysis and eligibility
forms; financial aid transcripts; copies of
documents submitted by students for
verification of student aid application
information; receipts; account cards;
copies of promissory notes; certifications
of enrollment status or other data to
grantors; and documentation of entrance
and exit interviews.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents UTMB's
participation in financial aid programs.
Records may include but are not limited
to: program participation agreements;
required reports; and any records
necessary to support the data contained in
the Fiscal Operations Report and
Application to Participate (FISAP).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents the
promissory notes for student loans
negotiated for the current academic year.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: If original promissory
notes are electronically signed, the
signed master promissory note
(MPN) must be kept 3 years after
all the loans made on the MPN are
satisfied.

series_description:
This records series documents the tuition
rebate forms and any supporting
documentation pertaining to a college
tuition rebate program.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
This records series documents statements
by students commenting on contested
information in a student record, or stating
why he or she disagrees with UTMB's
decision not to amend a record, or both.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents the process
of amending a student's record. Records
may include but are not limited to:
request to amend student record; notices
by an institution of denial or consent to
amendments; requests for hearings on
denied requests; hearing notices; and
written decisions by hearing examiners.

series_title:
Family Educational Rights
and Privacy Act (FERPA)
Documents
series_description:
This records series documents the process of
student information release requests and
consent authorizations or denials in accordance
with the Family Educational Rights and Privacy
Act (FERPA). Records may include but are not
limited to: request for access to and each
disclosure of personally identifiable information
from the educational records of a student;
records of disclosures made to third parties, an
official of the university for what the university
has determined are legitimate educational
interests, or a party seeking directory
information; requests for formal hearings;
student statements regarding hearing panel
decisions; written decisions of the hearing
panel; written consent of the eligible student or
student's parents to disclose records; waivers
for rights of access; student nondisclosure
requests to opt out of directory information
disclosure; waivers of access by students to
confidential letters and statements of
recommendation, and revocation of such
waiver; and any other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
This records series documents reports on
student academic standing, including
academic deficiency and the status
changes of academically deficient
students. Records may include but are not
limited to: reports containing student
names, grade point averages (GPAs), grade
point deficiencies, and numbers of
previous suspensions and probations;
student petitions for re-evaluation; report
of student progress toward academic
readmission/removal of probation status
at other institutions; academic honors and
awards; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.

This records series documents students
enrolled in courses taught, and is used to
cross-check those students who have
enrolled against those who have
registered, as well as in the generation of
statistical reports.
retention_statement: Permanent
legal_citation:
American Association
of Collegiate
Registrars and
Admissions Officers
(AACRAO) Student
Records
Management Guide.

series_description:
This records series documents Title IX
complaints, investigations, and
determination of responsibility, including
informal resolution or appeal.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
This records series documents abuse
and/or neglect findings observed by
medical staff during patient care. The
findings may be incident/variance reports
and evidence of child, domestic, elder, or
other kinds of abuse.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents all
necessary information for the completion
of the birth certificate including baby's
name, weight, height, time/date/location
of birth and information on the baby's
parents. The record copy of the birth
certificate is retained by the Office of Vital
Statistics.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 21 years

series_description:
This records series documents records of
radiation area surveys performed on
patients treated with radioactive
materials.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
This records series documents disclosures
and policies or procedures governing the
protection of Protected Health
Information (PHI) in accordance with the
Health Information Portability and
Accountability Act (HIPAA).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years

series_description:
This records series documents applications
submitted on behalf of a patient to a drug
company's or medical institution's
assistance program.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
This records series documents the United
States Food and Drug Administration (FDA)
and manufacturer recalls on drugs.
Records may include but are not limited
to: reportable occurrences of drug use;
recalls through the manufacturer; and
responses by departments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents forensic
autopsies. Records may include but are
not limited to: reports; slides; blocks;
photographs/negatives; and accession
logs.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
CAUTION: This series applies only
to inquests into a death occurring
in a county that does not have a
medical examiner's office or that is
not part of a medical examiner's
district.
legal_citation:
College of American
Pathologists (CAP)
and Clinical
Laboratory
Improvement
Amendments (CLIA)
Recommendation.

series_description:
This records series documents health
assessment and screening records that are
performed by a licensed professional,
performed outside of a regular treatment
facility, and not entered into the patient
medical record (e.g., cholesterol
screening, blood pressure testing, etc.).
Records may include but are not limited
to: tests; goals and objectives; diagnostic
reports; questionnaires; permission to
forward the information to the patient's
primary care physician; and any other
related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Health Referrals - Denied
or Not Seen
series_description:
This records series documents instances
where outpatients are referred to the
institution by non-institutional
practitioners who are denied health-
related services or who are not seen by
the institution.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
This records series documents lists of
potential donors who have been deferred
indefinitely due to abnormal test results.
retention_statement: Permanent
legal_citation:
American Association
of Blood Banks
(AABB) Standards for
Blood Banks and
Transfusion Services
(BB/TS), Current
Edition, Standard
6.2a.

series_description:
This records series documents everything
necessary to fully disclose Medicaid
reimbursable services provided. Records
may include but are not limited to:
eligibility screening; emergency Medicaid
coverage forms; case information release
forms; financial records relating to
program services; and medical release
physicians' statements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
legal_citation:
1 TAC 354.1004;
Texas Medicaid
Provider Procedures
Manual, Section
1.7.3.

series_title:
Medical Device Recall
Notices and Responses
series_description:
This records series documents the United
States Food and Drug Administration
(FDA), Safe Medical Devices Act (SMDA),
and manufacturer recalls on all medical,
food and drug devices. Records may
include but are not limited to: reportable
occurrences on devices; recalls through
the manufacturer; and responses by
UTMB departments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents a log of
patients visiting the health institution
(both in-patients and out-patients) and
patient encounters. The log may be used
to create annual census reports and 3-year
census comparisons. Records may include
but are not limited to: the date and time
that the patient came in; appointment;
admission; transport; dispatch; the
physician assigned; diagnosis; discharge;
patient photo; patient service; length of
stay; case logs; and remarks.

This records series documents a log of
various medical and patient related
activities that occur in the Emergency
Services department. Records may include
but are not limited to: Blue and Red Books
used for tub room care; admission,
transfer, and discharge logs; or any other
related documentation or
correspondence.

This records series documents hospital
transfer referral requests or interfacility
transfer requests. Records may include but
are not limited to: medical records and
other records of the individuals
transferred to or from the hospital;
referral intake forms; referral logs; and
recorded line conversations of transfers.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Medical Logs - Tissue and
Implant Logs
series_description:
This records series documents a log of
implants and tissue per patient, doctor,
and case date in the event recalls or issues
regarding the implant occurs.

This records series documents a log of
medical studies given to or completed
within the Office of Regulated Non-Clinical
Studies (ORNcS). Medical study logs may
include but are not limited to: name of
study; individual or individuals conducting
the study; type of study; or any other
related information about the study.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_description:
This records series documents tests
performed on patient and research
samples in the Electronic Microscopy Lab.
Records may include patient logbook.
comments:
Retention is based on Department
Policy (Medical Research).

series_description:
This records series documents non-
forensic autopsies, including reports,
slides, and blocks.
retention_statement: Permanent
legal_citation:
College of American
Pathologists (CAP)
and Clinical
Laboratory
Improvement
Amendments (CLIA)
recommendation.

series_title:
Pharmacy Records -
Prescription and Inventory
- TDCJ
series_description:
This records series documents TDCJ
prescription slips and other records of the
purchase, acquisition, inventory, disposal,
or dispensation of drugs and controlled
substances as required by federal law and
regulations, state law, and the rules of the
Texas State Board of Pharmacy (TSBP).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: See legal citations for
special filing requirements for
controlled substance and electronic
prescriptions.

series_title:
Pharmacy Records -
Prescription and Inventory
- Outpatient
series_description:
This records series documents outpatient
prescription slips and other records of the
purchase, acquisition, inventory, disposal,
or dispensation of drugs and controlled
substances as required by federal law and
regulations, state law, and the rules of the
Texas State Board of Pharmacy (TSBP).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
comments:
CAUTION: See legal citations for
special filing requirements for
controlled substance and electronic
prescriptions.

series_description:
This records series documents a record of
calls made to the Poison Control Center
documenting individual poisoning or
suspected poisoning incidents. Records
may include but are not limited to: case
sheets; date/time called; location of call;
case number; name of individual;
ingestion; treatment; and medical
outcome.

This records series documents health
information stored in any original media.
Records include fetal monitoring strips.
The Unit Medical Record (UMR) must
contain a written interpretation of all
source data. Source Data is distinct from
the written interpretations of significant
clinical information that has been
forwarded to the UMR.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 21 years

series_description:
This records series documents health
information stored on original media that
is not a part of the official medical record.
Records may include but are not limited
to: paper diagnostic tests or tools; x-rays;
videotapes and film; ultrasounds; fetal
monitoring strips; EKGs; photographs; and
any other related documentation or
correspondence not included in the
patient medical record.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_description:
This records series documents health
information stored in any original media.
Records may include but are not limited
to: mammography exams performed on a
patient; films filed in the patient's image
record; original order; and any other
related documentation or
correspondence. The Unit Medical Record
(UMR) must contain a written
interpretation of all source data. Source
Data is distinct from the written
interpretations of significant clinical
information that has been forwarded to
the UMR.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years
legal_citation:
25 TAC 289.230(t)(4);
Texas Health and
Safety Code,
401.424(a)(4)(E)(i).

series_description:
This records series documents the
university copies of information relating to
the selection by students of health
insurance offered to students through
UTMB. Records may include but are not
limited to: list of students that have been
charged the liability premiums;
documentation stating that students are
covered by the blanket liability policies;
health insurance waivers documenting
approval to opt out of university offered
insurance options; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents the
sterilization of surgical instruments used
by the health institution. Records may
include but are not limited to: autoclave
recording charts and log sheets indicating
date; load number; items sterilized; and
temperature/time settings.

series_title:
Pre-packing Cards and
Compounding Records for
Medications
series_description:
This records series documents the pre-
packing and compounding of medications.
Records may include pre-packing cards
and compounding worksheets (sterile and
non-sterile).

This records series documents a log of
specimens received daily from patients
which records the accession number,
name of patient, requesting doctor,
collection date, patient type, birth date,
and patient number.

series_title:
Solid Organ and Tissue
Tracking Records
series_description:
This records series documents the receipt
and disposition of all deceased donor and
external living non-UTMB donor organs
and tissues transplanted within the
hospital. Records may include but are not
limited to: solid organ or tissue type; the
donor id number; the name and license
number of the procurement or
distribution facility which supplied the
tissue/organ; recipient name and id
number; name of transplanting doctor;
date the organ/tissue was received by the
hospital; and the date of the transplant.
This information may be provided
quarterly to an organ or tissue
procurement service.

This records series documents the social
work activities at UTMB. Records may
include but are not limited to: dates of
social work services; types of social work
services; documentation of an assessment,
evaluation, or diagnosis of a client; and
billing information.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
CAUTION: Records are maintained
as a separate record and are not
interfiled within the patient
medical record.

series_description:
This records series documents medical
forensic sexual assault examinations, child
abuse, and domestic violence. Records
may include but are not limited to:
consent forms for advocates; digital
images; morning after pill; Sexual Assault
Nurse Examiner (SANE) discharge
instructions; patient consent; secure
digital forensic images; and any other
related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
This records series documents healthcare
case management records compiled from
patients. Records may include but are not
limited to: patient demographic
information; patient diagnosis;
medications; counseling notes; risk
reduction plans; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
This records series documents early
childhood intervention records. Records
may include but are not limited to: intake
information; assessments; releases;
diagnoses; plans; and progress notes of
intervention by all staff, including
occupational therapists, physical
therapists, speech pathologists,
nutritionists, early intervention specialists,
and social workers. May also contain
medical or other intervention records
from other early childhood intervention
programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
This records series documents the physical
examination of a Federal Aviation
Administration (FAA) applicant where
periodic monitoring of health or fitness is
required. Records may include but are not
limited to: FAA Certification Physicals; Fit-
For-Duty exams; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
legal_citation:
22 TAC 165.1(b)(1);
FAA Guide for
Aviation Medical
Examiners.

series_title:
Commercial Spaceflight
Physicals and Health
Assessments
series_description:
This records series documents physical
examinations of a Commercial Spaceflight
applicant where periodic monitoring of
health or fitness is required. Records may
include but are not limited to: Commercial
Spaceflight Physicals, Fit-For-Duty exams;
and any other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
This records series documents the profiles
for chemotherapy patients that tell what
drugs are given, the amount of each drug
given, the date and time given and, who
administered the drugs.
comments:
CAUTION: Medication profiles are
also documented in the prisoner
patient record. Prisoner patient
records are managed by TDCJ.

series_description:
This records series documents interactive
healthcare utilizing modern technology
and telecommunications to treat and
diagnosis remote patients live over video
for immediate care. Records may include
but are not limited to: video/still images;
patient data; consults; referrals; clinic
notes; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_description:
This records series documents the receipt
and evaluation of imaging services by
transmitting digitalized images from
routine x-rays, angiography, CT, MRI, PET
scanning, ultrasonography, and other
imaging devices by satellite or telephone
cabling to radiologists located at a
distance from the imaging site. Records
may include image exchange and any
other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 5 years

series_description:
This records series documents medical
opinions and advice provided by UTMB
physicians to organizations outside of the
University.

This records series documents air
ambulance records dispatched by
Emergency Room communications.
Records may include but are not limited
to: dispatch sheet; air transport record;
and follow-up notes.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 20 years

series_title:
Physician Advisor Review,
Approval, or Appeal
series_description:
This records series documents approval or
adverse determinations/appeals of each
prison unit's healthcare provider request
for specialist medical care that did or did
not meet medical necessity per InterQual
criteria and physician advisor review.
Records may include but are not limited
to: the signed Physician Advisor review
form; letter of adverse determination;
instructions for appeal; pertinent medical
information; and utilization review
committee determination.

This records series documents the referral
and acceptance of indigent patients to a
UTMB clinic from contracted counties
within the state. Records may include but
are not limited to: clinical information
referral form; clinic appointment
information; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents a log of the
initials or identification codes which
identifies pharmacy personnel by name.
The initials or identification code shall be
unique to ensure that each person can be
identified in connection with pharmacy
transactions.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
This records series documents the
monitoring, inspection, measurement,
repair, and illumination of antenna,
antenna supporting structures, and
transmitters authorized to operate with a
power output in excess of two watts.

This records series documents the
registration completed by UTMB students,
faculty, and staff for use of bicycles on
campus. The information collected on the
registration may include but not be limited
to: owner/user names; addresses;
telephone numbers; bicycle frame serial
numbers; bicycle models; and permit
numbers.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents the annual
fire safety report. The information on the
report may include but is not limited to:
fire statistics; fire drill data; fire safety
education policies and procedures;
policies or rules on portable electronic
appliances; procedures for student
housing evacuation; data regarding the
nature, date, time, and general location of
fires occurring in on-campus student
housing facilities; plans for future fire
safety improvements; and any other
related documentation or
correspondence.

This records series documents the fire log
and statistics on the number of fires, the
number of persons who received fire-
related injuries, the number of deaths
related to the fire, and the property
damage caused by fire for each on-campus
student housing facility.

This records series documents emergency
notifications that are distributed via e-
mail, text message, or other method to
the campus community regarding
emergencies, evacuations, timely warnings
related to public safety, weather related
instructions, shelter in place directives, or
other similar emergency situations.

series_title:
Clery Act Reporting -
Annual Security Report
series_description:
This records series documents the annual
security report created pursuant to the
Clery Act that summarizes statistics on the
nature, date, time, general location, and
disposition of the complaint (if known) of
crimes investigated by campus police.

series_title:
Clery Act Reporting -
Crime Log and Statistics
series_description:
This records series documents a crime log
and statistics on crimes investigated by
campus police or crimes occurring on
campus, in or on non-campus buildings or
property, and on public property pursuant
to the Clery Act. Crimes may include but
are not limited to: criminal homicide; sex
offenses; robbery; aggravated assault;
burglary; motor vehicle theft; arson; and
arrests for liquor law violations and illegal
weapons possession.

This records series documents daily
routine informational communications for
public safety officers and personnel on
duty. Records may include but are not
limited to: daily blotter; weather
advisories; hazardous conditions; and
reminders about "be on the lookout for"
(BOLO) notifications.

This records series documents emergency
protective orders issued by magistrates.
These orders include but are not limited
to: orders issued on the magistrate's own
motion or on the request of the victim of
the offense, the guardian of the victim, a
peace officer, or the attorney representing
the state; or a protective order issued
after the arrest for an offense involving
family violence, if the offense involved
serious bodily injury to the victim or in
which the arrest is for an offense that
involved the use or exhibition of a deadly
weapon during the commission of an
assault.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
legal_citation:
Texas Family Code,
85.025; Code of
Criminal Procedure,
17.292(j).

series_title:
Internal Affairs
Investigation Records -
Police Complaints and
Misconduct
series_description:
This records series documents complaints
filed by employees or the public against
police officers or the initiation,
investigation, and disposition of internal
affairs investigations of alleged
misconduct by law enforcement officers.
Records may include but are not limited
to: written complaints; internal
investigation reports; sworn statements
and affidavits; photos; evidence;
disciplinary reports; legal opinions and
advice; internal investigations whose
findings were not sustained or the accused
is exonerated; investigations that result in
informal discipline; records of
investigations that result in sustained
formal discipline (i.e., disciplinary action at
or above the level of a written reprimand);
records of investigations of law
enforcement shooting incidents which
result in death or injury to any person,
including the police officer; and any other
related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
This records series documents reports on
missing children and adults. Records may
include but are not limited to: requests for
investigation; photographs; fingerprints;
dental records; x-rays; notifications of
possible match; and similar documents
relating to the location of missing children
and adults, or similar records relating to
unidentified bodies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_description:
This records series documents a record of
horses, dogs, or other animals utilized by
campus police. Records may include but
are not limited to: records of the
acquisition of the animal; its registration
and pedigree papers; records of training;
and its veterinary history.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
This records series documents the
disposition of police weapons, which
includes buy-back programs, trade-ins, and
upgrades with vendors.

This records series documents the
inspection and repair of weapons.
retention_statement: Life of Asset

series_title:
Juvenile Case Records -
Not Referred to Court
series_description:
This records series documents juvenile
records that have not been referred to
juvenile court. Records may include but
are not limited to: reports; photos;
fingerprint records; and any other
supporting documentation needed for the
case.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Juvenile Case Records -
Referred to Court
series_description:
This records series documents juvenile
case records that have been referred to
juvenile court. Records may include but
are not limited to: offense reports;
warning notices; fingerprints;
photographs; and other records relating to
the investigation of an offense committed
by a juvenile and the taking of a juvenile
into custody in those instances in which a
warning notice has been issued and the
case has been referred to juvenile court.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 25 years
comments:
CAUTION: Records can be
destroyed prior to retention period
on order of destruction or
expungement granted by the court.

series_description:
This records series documents the
assistance given to students in planning
career goals and connecting with
prospective employers. Records may
include but are not limited to: career
goals; academic credentials; personal
data; work experience; honors;
distinctions; consent forms for release of
information; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Child and Youth Program
Staff Records
series_description:
This series documents the staff
applications for child and youth programs,
background check reports, and training
documentation.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: If the youth camp is
notified of an investigation or
conviction of a camp staff member
for an act of sexual abuse, as
defined by Section 21.02 of the
Texas Penal Code, which occurred
at the camp, the camp shall retain
all records related to the
investigation or conviction until the
department notifies the camp that
the record retention is no longer
required.

series_title:
Childcare Center Records -
Licensing, Safety, and
Compliance
series_description:
This records series documents childcare
center administrative records required by
the Texas Health and Human Services
Commission (HHSC).
comments:
CAUTION: Does not include
childcare center employee files.
Refer to RRS Section 3 for
personnel, training, and employee
attendance records.

series_title:
Non-University Student
Records - Special Interest
Courses - Senior Learner
series_description:
This records series documents receipt of
student registration/enrollment in non-
credit special interest courses and
activities (arts and crafts, dance, music and
other educational type classes) for senior
(over 55 years old) learners. Records may
include but are not limited to: registration
form; miscellaneous instructional material;
and any other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
This records series documents requests for
prayers made by individuals to the
Pastoral Care Department.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
This records series documents baptisms
performed by UTMB Pastoral Care clergy,
which may include a copy of the original
baptism certificate and a copy of the
original registration.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
This records series documents the daily
and routine administration of academic
programs, special academic programs, or
non-institution student programs of the
department or college. Records may
include but are not limited to: registration
reports; add-drop analyses and reports;
course enrollment summaries by class;
graduation summaries; majors by class
level; international activities; cooperative
ventures; summer term classes and
enrollment reports; placement
information; working papers; and any
other related documentation or
correspondence.

series_title:
Class Scheduling Records -
Published Schedule of
Classes
series_description:
This records series documents the
formulation of class schedules by
academic departments for inclusion in the
published schedule of classes. Records
may include the final published schedule
of classes and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
This records series documents the
formulation of class schedules by
academic departments for inclusion in the
published schedule of classes. Records
may include but are not limited to:
requests from departments for class
offerings; deviation from schedule forms;
copies of course schedule maintenance
forms; requests for class changes; working
papers; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.

This records series documents
commencement program planning and
implementation at the institution. Records
may include but are not limited to:
commencement attendance forms;
planning records created by
commencement committees or other
planning groups; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
This records series documents the
institution's participation in cooperative
and shared educational or research
programs. Such programs may share
research facilities and resources or
instructional programs such as programs
permitting student matriculation at
member institutions. Records may include
but are not limited to: information on
requirements and application procedures;
committee minutes; meeting agendas; and
memos of interpretation and
understanding.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_title:
Course Records - Medical
Treatment Plans /
Teaching Files
series_description:
This records series documents the medical
and dental treatment plans used primarily
for teaching purposes. Records may
include but are not limited to: copies of
patient work-up forms; diagnosis; a
treatment and management plan for each
patient that is seen over a 2 week period;
patient demographics; medication list;
patient needs risk assessment; and
insurance information may also be
included.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Degree and Special
Academic Program
Proposal, Development,
and Review Records
series_description:
This records series documents the
planning and discussions relating to the
implementation of new undergraduate,
advanced degree, and special academic
programs and any major reorganization or
changes to established programs. Records
may include but are not limited to: final
reports; curriculum committee meeting
minutes; curriculum proposals; reviews of
individual degree programs by campus and
off-campus sources; letters of support;
review agendas; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents and
corrects deficiencies during interim
periods between visits from accrediting
agencies and the Joint Commission on
Accreditation of Healthcare Organizations
(JCAHO). Records may include but are not
limited to: preliminary self studies and
surveys; planning documents used to
establish goals and indicators; temporary
problem solving team notes and
discussions; action plans; work
assignments; research; history of previous
scores / assignments; REF tool for
comprehensive assessment scoring;
quality reviews; Ad HOC projects assigned
to deficiencies; FSA compliance concerns;
data and deficiency tracking; and any
other related documentation or
correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
This records series documents records
relating to the care, management and
breeding of animals for research and
teaching purposes.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: If the research facility
has been notified in writing that
specified records shall be retained
pending completion of an
investigation or proceeding, the
research facility shall hold those
records until their disposition is
authorized in writing by the United
States Department of Agriculture
(USDA), Animal and Plant Health
Inspection Service (APHIS).

series_title:
Institutional Animal Care
and Use Committee
(IACUC) Records
series_description:
This records series documents the review
of research protocols and the evaluation
of UTMB's animal care and use during
laboratory research by the Institutional
Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
Records may include but are not limited
to: meeting minutes; records of
attendance; activities of the Committee;
Committee deliberations; records of
proposed activities involving animals and
proposed significant changes in activities
involving animals and whether IACUC
approval was given or withheld; and
semiannual IACUC reports and
recommendations (including minority
views).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
CAUTION: If the research facility
has been notified in writing that
specified records shall be retained
pending completion of an
investigation or proceeding, the
research facility shall hold those
records until their disposition is
authorized in writing by the United
States Department of Agriculture
(USDA), Animal and Plant Health
Inspection Service (APHIS).

series_description:
This records series documents the review,
approval, and monitoring of research
projects that involve biological agents and
chemicals that pose differing levels of
safety, health, or environmental risk.
Records may include but are not limited
to: meeting minutes; agendas; notes;
attendance records; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.

series_title:
UTMB Funded Research -
Minutes and Final
Research Reports
series_description:
This records series documents the
activities of the institutional councils and
boards, which review proposals and make
recommendations for awards to faculty
for research that is not otherwise
supported by organized or directed
programs but is designed to lead to other
funding sources. Examples of projects
funded may include but are not limited to:
pilot research; emergency funding;
emerging research opportunities; new
research field or new research field for
investigator; developing research
laboratories; and centrally shared research
resources. Records may include meeting
minutes and final research reports.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_title:
UTMB Funded Research -
Project Review Records
series_description:
This records series documents the
activities of the institutional councils and
boards, which review proposals and
project funding requirements to make
recommendations for awards to faculty
for research that is not otherwise
supported by organized or directed
programs but is designed to lead to other
funding sources. Examples of projects
funded may include but are not limited to:
pilot research; emergency funding;
emerging research opportunities; new
research field or new research field for
investigator; developing research
laboratories; and centrally shared research
resources. Records may include but are
not limited to: applicant case files;
agendas; notes; working papers; award
letters; and applications for research
support.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_title:
Patent and Invention
Records - Auxiliary Files
series_description:
This records series documents the transfer
of technology from the institution to
outside agencies as the result of research
projects and grants carried out at the
institution. Records may include but are
not limited to: patent applications;
agreements giving permission for
institutional researchers to use other
patented inventions in their research;
descriptions and titles of inventions;
sources of funding to create the
inventions; details of the provenance of
the inventions and their documentation;

or applications for patents that are either
not filed or otherwise pursued by UTMB,

series_description:

of observed, suspected, or apparent
research misconduct brought forward to
the Scientific Integrity Committee by or
against UTMB personnel and affiliates and
relating to research projects. Records may
include but are not limited to: allegations
of research misconduct; conflict of interest
forms; research misconduct investigations;
interview transcripts and/or recordings; all
research misconduct proceeding
documentation; committee meeting
minutes and notes; inquiry reports; final
investigation report; decision not to
investigate; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_title:

Clinical Trials - Not
Associated with Grants
series_description:
This records series documents research
data and documentation gathered or
created in the course of a clinical trial in
which UTMB is the primary sponsor.
Records may include but are not limited
to: case reports; shipping records;
research data from investigators; internal
research data; study protocols/designs
and amendments; FDA forms;
pharmaceutical studies; findings; research
papers; and serious adverse events
reports.
retention_statement: Permanent

Records may include but are not limited
to: the research services agreement with
the outside entity; any research data
collected and submitted to that outside
entity for its study; and any other related
documentation or correspondence.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

project funding. Records may include
correspondence and working papers.
